name: Devilled Eggs
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Tomatoes, skinned and chopped (or 2 x 14oz tinned tomatoes, chopped)
recipeInstructions:
Hot, spicy sauces - devils - were popular in Britain in the 19th century. This sauce is served with poached eggs but goes equally well with fried eggs. The sauce can be made the night before and kept covered in the fridge. Warm through just before serving. An egg poacher can be used.
1. Fry the onion in the butter for 3 minutes.
2. Add the tomatoes, lemon juice, half the vinegar, tomato puree, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, stock, parsley, and salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil and simmer gently for 20 minutes until fairly thick.
3. Keep the sauce warm.
4. Lightly toast and butter crumpets or muffins, and keep them warm.
5. Fill a deep frying pan with water. Add the remaining vinegar and bring to a gentle rolling boil.
6. One at a time, crack each egg into a cup, and slide the egg into the bubbling water. Poach the eggs for 3 minutes.
7. Using two spoons, gather in the egg whites as they start to set, to give each egg a neat shape.
8. Lift out the cooked eggs and drain on kitchen paper.
9. Place each crumpet or muffin on a plate, and top with an egg. Spoon over the hot sauce, and serve immediately.
